name: Triple Chocolate Mousse Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dark chocolate mousse layer:
a. Place the coffee and vanilla in a small cup. Sprinkle the gelatin over the mixture and let it soften for 5 minutes.
b. In a medium bowl, whisk the yolks until blended.
c. In a heavy saucepan, combine 3/4 cup of cream, milk, and sugar. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until small bubbles form around the side of the pan. Remove from heat and gradually whisk about 3/4 cup hot cream mixture into the yolks until blended. Pour this mixture back into the saucepan.
d. Continue cooking over medium-low heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon for 2 to 4 minutes, until the custard has thickened slightly. It is done when you can run your finger down the back of the coated spoon and a path remains in the custard for several seconds. Do not allow the custard to boil.
e. Remove from heat and strain into a stainless steel bowl. Add softened gelatin mixture and stir until dissolved. Add chocolate and stir until melted and smooth. Set over ice water and stir for 5 to 10 minutes, until cool. Remove from ice water.
2. Beat remaining cream in chilled medium bowl until soft peaks form. Fold one third of whipped cream into chocolate mixture. Fold in remaining whipped cream. Scrape mousse into pan and smooth into an even layer. Freeze for 1 hour, until set.
3. Make the milk chocolate mousse layer by following steps above with milk chocolate ingredients and pouring over dark chocolate mousse layer. Freeze for 1 hour until set.
4. Make white chocolate mousse layer:
a. Place rum, water, and vanilla in small heatproof cup. Sprinkle gelatin over mixture and soften for 5 minutes.
b. Heat gelatin mixture in hot water, stirring frequently for 3 to 5 minutes until dissolved and clear. Remove from heat.
c. Melt chocolate with warm gelatin mixture. Cool over ice water.
d. Beat cream until soft mounds form. Fold into white chocolate mixture. Scrape onto milk chocolate layer and freeze for 2 hours until set.
5. Unmold cake using hair dryer or damp towel. Transfer to serving platter and refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.
